name: home-assistant-blade-mcp
title: Home Assistant
description: Home Assistant operations — entities, state, history, device control, automations, energy, multi-site management
tagline: Multi-site Home Assistant control with write gating
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Entities & state — query any entity, filter by domain, area, or floor
History & energy — state history, energy monitoring, long-term statistics
Device control — call services, activate scenes, trigger automations
Automations — list, toggle, create, and manage automation rules
Multi-site — manage multiple HA instances from a single plugin
Write-gated — reads always work, device commands require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Home Assistant Blade gives your AI agent full operational control over one or more Home Assistant instances. 36 tools cover entity state queries, device control, automation management, energy monitoring, history analysis, and scene activation.

Your agent can check which lights are on, review energy consumption for the past week, trigger an automation, activate a scene, and manage smart home devices across multiple sites — all in a single conversation.

### Multi-site support

Unlike most Home Assistant integrations, this plugin supports multiple instances natively. Set `HA_PROVIDERS` to a comma-separated list of site names, then configure per-provider URL and token environment variables. Each tool call is scoped to a specific provider, so your agent can compare energy usage between two homes or check device status across sites.

### Token efficiency

Home Assistant's REST API returns verbose entity state payloads with deep attribute nesting. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts only the fields your agent needs. Entity lists are filtered by domain, area, or floor so you never waste context on irrelevant devices.

### Safety model

All 36 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — entity state, history, energy stats, automation list
- **Writes** require `HA_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — call services, activate scenes, toggle automations, create automations

This means your agent can monitor your home freely but cannot change anything without explicit permission.

env.name: HA_PROVIDERS
env.description: Comma-separated provider names for multi-instance mode (e.g. sandybay,paddington)

setup.blurb: Connect to Home Assistant for smart home control, monitoring, and automation management. Supports multi-site.
setup.help_text: Create a Long-Lived Access Token at Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens in your Home Assistant instance. For multi-site, set HA_PROVIDERS and per-provider URL/token env vars.

fields.label: Home Assistant URL
fields.help: The base URL of your Home Assistant instance

fields.label: Enable Write Operations
fields.help: Set to 'true' to enable device control, scene activation, and automation triggers

scenarios.label: Evening shutdown routine
scenarios.body: Check which lights and devices are still on across the house, turn off non-essential items, arm the security system, and confirm the final state.

scenarios.label: Weekly energy audit
scenarios.body: Pull energy consumption data for the past week, compare usage across areas, identify the highest-consuming devices, and suggest automation rules to reduce waste.

scenarios.label: Cross-site status check
scenarios.body: Query entity states across multiple Home Assistant instances, compare climate readings between sites, and flag any offline devices or triggered alarms.
